# Infra Rules

> Extends [root AGENTS.md](../AGENTS.md). Auto-loaded when editing `*.tf`, `*.yaml`, `Jenkinsfile`, `values/*`.

## Always

- Set `resources.requests` for every container (limits auto-calculated for servers). [MUST]
- Configure health probes for every deployment (default path `/` on port 6050). [MUST]
- Manage all GCP resources via Terraform — no manual console changes. [MUST]
- Use Jenkins shared library functions — not raw docker/helm/kubectl commands. [MUST]
- Use Helm values overrides per environment (dev/staging/prod). [SHOULD]
- Set `terminationGracePeriodSeconds` ≥ 30 seconds for graceful shutdown. [SHOULD]
- Pin image tags to a specific digest or semver — never use `latest`. [SHOULD]

## Never

- Never set CPU limits equal to CPU requests (causes CPU throttling on spikes). [MUST]
- Never store secrets in Helm values or ConfigMaps — use GCP Secret Manager. [MUST]
- Never run raw kubectl/helm commands in production — use Jenkins pipelines. [MUST]
- Never use KEDA — it is deprecated. Use `peakLoad.peakLoadMinReplicas` instead. [MUST]
- Never skip resource limits — unbounded containers can OOM the node. [MUST]

## Prefer

- Prefer `minAvailable: 1` PodDisruptionBudget for stateful services.
- Prefer blue-green deployment for breaking changes (Jenkins DeploymentOption, not kubectl).
- Prefer `peakLoad.peakLoadMinReplicas` for traffic spike handling.
- Prefer platform Terraform modules (local paths in `platform-backend/terraform/`).

## Verify

- Confirm `helm lint` passes before deploying.
- Confirm `terraform plan` reviewed before `terraform apply`.
- Confirm new secrets are registered in GCP Secret Manager, not Helm values.

## References

- [kubernetes-workloads](references/kubernetes-workloads.md) — Helm values, resource limits, probes
- [terraform-iac](references/terraform-iac.md) — module patterns, naming conventions
- [ci-cd-pipelines](references/ci-cd-pipelines.md) — Jenkins, blue-green deploy
- [observability](references/observability.md) — logging, tracing, metrics stack
